free_text: Cross section measurements of deuteron induced nuclear
reactions on natural tungsten up to 34 MeV.
A set of thin foils is irradiated containing 10 mum
thick tungsten targets, 10 mum thick titanium monitor
foils to have information on the beam flux and thick
aluminum degrader foils, between 100 and 500 mum, to
change the incident beam energy, which impinged the
Isotopic composition of natural Ti and W- foils-
30.7%, W-186 is 28.6%. The chemical purity of the
sample was taking in to account-99.95% for W and 99.6%
All foils were counted twice: starting the day after
the irradiation (for few hours)and after 2 or 3 weeks
